title: Migrate Weltgewebe legacy validation commands and duplicate preflights
goal: After consumer parity is proven, replace free-form validation_commands, legacy minimal parsers and duplicate Agent preflight surfaces in Weltgewebe with named fail-closed validation profiles and one canonical contract path, then remove only the superseded compatibility code.

approval.note: Migration may remove compatibility paths only after exact consumer inventory, parity tests and rollback evidence.

acceptance.assertion: Every consumer of validation_commands, legacy Agent contract parsers and duplicate preflight entrypoints is inventoried with exact repository path, invocation source and current compatibility requirement.
Named validation profiles cover every retained validation intent with deterministic argv, fail-closed result semantics and negative tests for omitted, unknown or softened checks.
Agent contract interpretation and preflight reach one canonical implementation; compatibility adapters are thin projections and cannot establish an independent truth or execution authority.
Superseded parsers and free-form command fields are removed only after current-main CI, fresh-session consumer proof and rollback fixtures pass; no unrelated task or runtime behavior changes.

rollback.strategy: Restore the compatibility adapters while retaining the canonical agent-contract.json schema and named profile definitions; never restore a generic repository write operator.

metadata.source_finding: Legacy validation_commands, minimal parsers and duplicate preflight surfaces remain intentionally transitional after the canonical Agent contract merge.
does_not_establish: permission to remove compatibility code before proof
